items.title: Interstate fans say keep Gather Round in Adelaide as AFL boss probed on why AFLW not part of the program

children.content: Crows AFLW player Ebony Marinoff has questioned the AFL's CEO on why the women's program hasn't been part of the successful Gather Round in Adelaide.

articles.title: 'He didn't want to speak to me': Port star speaks after report over language

children.content: Zak Butters defends himself after the Power star was reported for using inappropriate language towards an umpire.

image.altText: GettyImages-2270971785

articles.title: Fans living it up in Adelaide for Gather Round but future of event uncertain

children.content: Occupancy rates and food and drink sales are up on last year's Gather Round, says the Australian Hotels Association, despite concerns fuel prices would deter interstate visitors.

image.altText: People in different club attires walk on a footbridge with Gather Round signs. A lit up Adelaide Oval in the distance at night

items.title: Nick Xenophon-founded SA Best party comes to an end

children.content: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

image.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair standing with a cut-out of a man wearing an orange vest

items.title: Independent supermarkets struggle to keep prices down as Iran war hurts

children.content: Suppliers are charging more to deliver groceries to supermarkets as fuel prices increase, putting pressure on smaller retailers.

image.altText: Fresh vegetables on three black shelves with price stickers in front of the items.

items.title: Handwritten church music 'caked in dust' performed after 50 years

children.content: A South Australian church celebrated 150 years with a special performance of sacred music transcribed by early-20th-century parishioners.

gallery.altText: A man with glasses and a blue coat picking up sheet music from inside a white, rusty chest

thumbnail.altText: A man with glasses and a blue coat picking up sheet music from inside a white, rusty chest

items.title: Hiker found 'safe and well' after spending 24 hours lost in Victoria's north-west

children.content: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

image.altText: Police finds missing camper Andy

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, as owners raise concerns

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

A small brick church among gum trees
A brick and wood house among trees

A small brick church among gum trees

items.title: SA GP with an 'unhealthy obsession' for laser therapy disqualified

children.content: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

image.altText: Close-up photo of a silver and white stethoscope. Dec 2022.

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

image.altText: Cars fill a freeway

items.title: 'She just lost every coin flip': How a short hike went so wrong for Celine Cremer

children.content: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime, but on a hike in Tasmania she left the trail and was never seen again. It took a private investigator, a YouTuber, loved ones and an army of volunteers to find answers.

gallery.altText: A green background with a composite of two images- a young woman and a slice of satellite map indicating locations

thumbnail.altText: A green background with a composite of two images- a young woman and a slice of satellite map indicating locations

items.title: The underground 3D printing farms arming Ukraine's soldiers

children.content: In spare rooms and backyard sheds, volunteers across Europe are 3D printing drone bomb parts to supply Ukraine's military.

image.altText: A man in camoflague with his face covered sits between boxes of 3D printed military equipment.

items.title: 'Like a flea on an elephant's back': The unexpected rise of solar power

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

images.altText: Rory McIlroy raises his arms after winning the Masters.

children.content: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta. Follow live.

items.title: Fuel crisis has some some delaying retirement and working from home

children.content: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran and spiking fuel prices have created similar struggles.

image.altText: A woman sitting with a guitar looking away from the camera.

items.title: Australia has not been asked to participate in US's Hormuz blockade: PM

children.content: Australia has not received a request to participate in US President Donald Trump's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Anthony Albanese says. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese stands in front of Australian and Indigenous flag and gesture while speaking. Wears blue suit, lime green tie.

items.title: Heroes of Bondi stabbing awarded for their bravery two years on

items.title: Caretaker files for possession of impressionist Frederick McCubbin's former country home

children.content: A court has heard claims the former home of an Australian impressionist is not safe to walk through, with "black mould, evidence of vermin and wheelie bins collecting rainwater".

image.altText: A two-storey green house with brick chimneys.

items.title: Markets on edge, oil set to rise on US Gulf blockade threats

children.content: The Middle East failed peace talks are expected to drive oil prices higher and reverse the anticipated gains on the ASX today. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

children.content: What should be done with all those used tennis balls that get inevitably sent to landfill? Lilian Xu might have found the answer.

items.title: 'Missed opportunity': Astronomers lament loss of telescope partnership

children.content: The Artemis II mission used space technology developed in Australia, but experts fear the axing of a multimillion-dollar telescope partnership may stifle innovation and astronomy.

image.altText: Five buildings, four of which are firing lasers from their rooftops, against a starry Milky Way backdrop.

items.title: Widower of woman killed in front yard demands answers after trial abandoned

children.content: Mick Fairfield arrived home after a normal day at work to find his wife dead with stab wounds and his young daughter hiding under a blanket. He waited years for the case to go to trial, but it never did, and now he wants answers.

items.title: Mystery surrounds suspected people smuggler in WA's far-north

children.content: Months after the arrival of six Chinese nationals in one of Australia's most isolated communities, questions remain about another man suspected of attempting to assist them.

items.title: Domestic violence hotline can only answer 50 per cent of calls

children.content: More than $290 million in federal funding is on the table for domestic violence services nationwide, but Canberra providers are concerned about how it will be distributed and tracked by the ACT government.

items.title: Major setback for beekeepers as varroa mite chemical resistance detected again

children.content: There are growing fears that a second varroa mite strain could enter Australia, as beekeepers lose key chemical options for controlling the tiny parasites of honey bees.

items.title: North Queensland set for more rain as ex-Cyclone Maila heads for coast

children.content: Beautiful one day, heavy rain, dangerous surf and frosts, the next — Queensland faces a mixed bag of weather throughout the state over the coming days.

items.title: Loved, but costly — is the end near for this heritage railway?

children.content: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

items.title: Victorian government tried to keep major housing budget blowout under wrap

items.title: Cyclone Maila kills at least 11 people in Papua New Guinea

children.content: The Australian government will give $2.5 million to support relief and recovery efforts in Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands after Tropical Cyclone Maila.

items.title: The reality of living without a car in Brisbane

children.content: As the cost of owning a car goes up, some Brisbane residents are choosing to live without one. But going car-free isn't possible for everyone.

items.title: Youth mental health centre to close amid budget crisis

children.content: A NSW South Coast community says the proposed closure of a mental health and welfare centre could result in more youth suicides.
